description: A corpus for modern API back-ends, covering attack classes the built-in corpus does not: NoSQL operator injection (MongoDB-style $ne/$gt/$where) and GraphQL abuse (deeply nested queries, alias-based batching). Malicious entries are generic, well-documented structural shapes of each class; benign entries are normal API payloads that merely resemble them. Use a custom ruleset (see nosql-rules.json) tuned for JSON/GraphQL bodies.

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: operator injection: $ne:null turns a password check into 'not null'

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: $gt with empty string matches all string values

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: $where executes server-side JavaScript against the document

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: $regex used to brute-force a secret one character at a time

entries.label: benign
entries.note: a normal credential payload, no query operators

entries.label: benign
entries.note: free text that mentions a dollar sign and 'greater'; no operator key

entries.label: malicious
entries.text: query { a { b { c { d { e { f { g { h { id } } } } } } } } }
entries.note: deeply nested query designed to exhaust the resolver (DoS)

entries.label: malicious
entries.text: { a1: login(u:"x",p:"1") a2: login(u:"x",p:"2") a3: login(u:"x",p:"3") }
entries.note: alias-based batching to brute-force credentials in one request

entries.label: benign
entries.text: { featured: product(id: 1) { name } newest: product(id: 2) { name } }
entries.note: legitimate use of two aliases to fetch two products
